chapter: Chapter: Weavers, Iron Smelters and Factory Owners

questions.question: Why were Indian textiles in great demand in international markets?
questions.answer: Indian textiles were known for their fine quality, bright colors and beautiful designs.

questions.question: Why did the East India Company control the work of Indian weavers?
questions.answer: The Company wanted to ensure a steady supply of cloth for trade.

questions.question: What role did gomasthas play in the textile trade?
questions.answer: They supervised weavers and ensured they supplied cloth to the Company.

questions.question: Why were weavers unhappy with the gomasthas?
questions.answer: Gomasthas forced weavers to sell cloth only to the Company at low prices.

questions.question: What were advances given to weavers by the Company?
questions.answer: Advances were loans given to weavers to produce cloth for the Company.

questions.question: Why did many weavers fall into debt under Company rule?
questions.answer: Because they had to repay advances even when cloth prices were low.

questions.question: How did the Industrial Revolution affect textile production?
questions.answer: It introduced machines that increased cloth production in factories.

questions.question: Why did machine-made cloth affect Indian weavers?
questions.answer: Machine-made cloth was cheaper and replaced handwoven textiles.

questions.question: What was the power loom?
questions.answer: It was a machine used in factories to weave cloth quickly.

questions.question: Why did the demand for Indian textiles decline in the nineteenth century?
questions.answer: Because machine-made textiles from Britain flooded the market.

questions.question: How did the British control the production of cloth in India?
questions.answer: They used gomasthas and gave advances to weavers.

questions.question: Why did some weavers migrate to villages?
questions.answer: To escape the strict control of Company agents.

questions.question: What raw material was most important for Indian textile production?
questions.answer: Cotton was the most important raw material.

questions.question: Why were Indian weavers skilled artisans?
questions.answer: They had traditional knowledge of spinning and weaving techniques.

questions.question: How did industrialization change textile production?
questions.answer: Production shifted from handlooms to machines in factories.

questions.question: Why did British factories produce large quantities of cloth?
questions.answer: Because machines increased speed and efficiency of production.

questions.question: Why were textile mills established in Indian cities?
questions.answer: To produce cloth using machines for growing markets.

questions.question: Name one city where textile mills developed in India.
questions.answer: Bombay became an important center for textile mills.

questions.question: How did the Company ensure that weavers worked only for them?
questions.answer: They controlled production through contracts and advances.

questions.question: What was the impact of British trade policies on Indian weavers?
questions.answer: It reduced their income and limited their freedom to sell cloth.

questions.question: Why did traditional weaving industries decline in India?
questions.answer: Because of competition from machine-made textiles.

questions.question: How did factory production differ from traditional weaving?
questions.answer: Factory production used machines and produced cloth in large quantities.

questions.question: Why did British cloth become cheaper than Indian cloth?
questions.answer: Machines reduced production costs in factories.

questions.question: What happened to many Indian weavers during industrialization?
questions.answer: Many lost their traditional livelihood and became laborers.

questions.question: What major change did industrialization bring to the textile industry?
questions.answer: It replaced handloom weaving with factory-based production.
